text: On the island of knights and knaves, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. You meet three inhabitants: A says "I am a knight," B says "A is a knave," and C says "B is a knave." Determine the types of A, B, and C.
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "We are both knaves" (referring to himself and B). B says nothing. What are A and B?

Five people — Ann, Bob, Cal, Dee, Ena — sit in a row. Ann is not at either end. Bob sits immediately left of Cal. Dee sits at one of the ends. Ena is not adjacent to Ann. There are exactly two people between Bob and Dee. Who sits at each position?

Three suspects X, Y, Z each make one statement. X says "Y is guilty." Y says "X is lying." Z says "Y is telling the truth." Exactly one person is guilty and only the guilty person lies. Who is guilty?

On the island of knights and knaves, four inhabitants A, B, C, D make statements. A says "Exactly one of us is a knave." B says "Exactly two of us are knaves." C says "A and B are both knaves." D says "C is a knave." How many knaves are there and who are they?
Alice, Bob, Carol, and Dave each own a different pet: a cat, a dog, a fish, and a parrot. Alice does not own the cat or the fish. Bob owns the dog. Carol does not own the parrot. Who owns what?

Six athletes finish a race in distinct places 1 through 6. Ana finishes before Bea. Cara finishes immediately after Bea. Dan finishes last. Ed finishes before Ana. Fay finishes immediately before Ed. List the finishing order.
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "B and I are the same type." B says "That is false." What types are A and B?

Four cards each show a letter on one side and a number on the other. The rule is: if a card shows a vowel, then it has an even number on the other side. The visible faces are A, K, 4, 7. Which cards must you flip to test the rule?

In a group of five people, each person either always tells the truth or always lies. Ava says "Exactly three of us tell the truth." Ben says "Ava is lying." Cal says "Ben is telling the truth." Dana says "Cal is lying." Eve says "Dana is telling the truth." How many truth-tellers are there?
Three boxes are labeled "Apples," "Oranges," and "Mixed." All labels are wrong. You draw one fruit from the box labeled "Mixed" and it is an apple. What is in each box?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "At least one of us is a knave" and B says "A is a knight." What are A and B?

Seven people must be seated at a round table with seven seats. Pat must sit next to Quinn. Ray must not sit next to Sam. Tam sits directly opposite Uma. Val sits next to Tam. Ray sits next to Val. Given these constraints, does Sam sit next to Quinn? Answer yes or no and justify.

Alice, Bob, and Carol each have a different favorite color: red, blue, or green. Alice says "My favorite is not red." Bob says "My favorite is blue." Carol says "Bob's favorite is not blue." Exactly one of them is lying. What is each person's favorite color?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "I am a knave or B is a knight." B says "A is a knight." What are A and B?

Five houses in a row are painted red, blue, green, yellow, and white in some order. The red house is immediately left of the blue house. The green house is not at either end. The yellow house is immediately right of the green house. The white house is at one end. Determine the order of the houses.
A says "B is a knight." B says "C is a knave." C says "A is a knave." On the island of knights and knaves, all three statements are made. How many knights are there and who are they?

Four students — W, X, Y, Z — each study a different subject: math, physics, chemistry, biology. W does not study math or physics. X does not study chemistry. Y studies biology. Z does not study biology or math. Who studies what?
On the island of knights and knaves, five inhabitants each say "The majority of us are knaves." How many are knights and how many are knaves?

Two envelopes each contain a number. You are told one number is exactly twice the other. You open one envelope and see 10. Should you switch to maximize expected value, and why does the naive expected-value argument break down? Answer by identifying the logical flaw.

Three friends Pia, Quin, and Rex each make two statements about a single hidden number between 1 and 9. Pia says "The number is greater than 5" and "The number is even." Quin says "The number is less than 7" and "The number is odd." Rex says "The number is not 6" and "The number is greater than 4." Each friend has exactly one true and one false statement. What is the number?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "Exactly two of us three are knights," B says "Exactly one of us three is a knight," and C says "A is a knave." What type is each person?

Six colored balls — red, orange, yellow, green, blue, violet — are placed in a line. Red is not adjacent to orange. Yellow is immediately between orange and green. Blue is at one end. Violet is not adjacent to blue. Green is immediately left of blue. Determine the order.

Alicia, Bruno, and Carla are each assigned a role: detective, suspect, or witness (all different). Alicia says "I am not the suspect." Bruno says "Carla is the detective." Carla says "I am not the detective." Exactly one of them lies. Who holds which role?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "B is a knave," B says "C is a knave," and C says "A and B are both knaves." Determine the type of each.
Four friends each prefer a different season: spring, summer, autumn, winter. Amy does not prefer summer or winter. Ben prefers autumn. Carl does not prefer spring. Who prefers what?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "I am a knight and B is a knave." B says "At least one of us is a knight." What are A and B?

Five suspects in a crime each make one statement. Aaron says "I did not do it." Beth says "Aaron is telling the truth." Cass says "Beth is lying." Dale says "Cass is telling the truth." Eve says "Dale is lying." Exactly one person is the criminal and only the criminal lies. Who is the criminal?
On the island of knights and knaves, A says "B is a knight," B says "A and C are different types," and C says "B is a knave." Determine the type of each.

Three logicians — Sol, Tan, Ula — each know their own hat color (red or blue) but not the others'. Sol sees Tan and Ula's hats. Tan sees only Ula's hat. Ula sees no hats. Sol says "I cannot determine my hat color." Tan says "I cannot determine my hat color." Ula then says "I know my hat color." What color is Ula's hat?
